title: amaranthus blitum subsp. emarginatus
intro: Amaranthus blitum subsp. emarginatus, also known as velvet flower or red amaranth, is a subspecies of the Amaranthus blitum plant, a type of flowering plant native to Europe, Asia, and Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Amaranthus blitum subsp. emarginatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as an antioxidant, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be made by drying the leaves and flowers, then steeping them in hot water or mixing with a carrier oil for external use.
